nodes.label: Finetune SAM README
nodes.author: Hanxue Gu, Haoyu Dong, Jichen Yang, Maciej A. Mazurowski
nodes.rationale: Comprehensive empirical study of fine-tuning SAM for medical imaging across 18 strategy combinations (3 encoders x 2 components x 3 PEFT methods) and 17 datasets; key findings: PEFT in both encoder+decoder outperforms other strategies; SSL pretraining improves downstream performance; network architecture has small impact

nodes.label: SAM Original Pretrained Weights (Facebook)
nodes.rationale: Segment Anything Model, the base foundation model; normalisation: SA-1B statistics (mean=[0.485,0.456,0.406], std=[0.229,0.224,0.225]); paper: arxiv 2304.02643; weights downloadable from GitHub model checkpoints

nodes.label: MedSAM Pretrained Weights
nodes.rationale: SAM fine-tuned on broad medical imaging data; normalisation: min-max [0,1] x 255 (differs from SAM, requires medsam normalize_type); published in Nature Communications

nodes.label: MobileSAM Pretrained Weights
nodes.rationale: Lightweight SAM with ViT-Tiny (depth=4) encoder produced via knowledge distillation from ViT-H; enables fine-tuning on limited compute

nodes.label: SSL-SAM Pretrained Weights
nodes.rationale: Self-supervised pretrained SAM encoder from Setup 3 (task-agnostic pretraining on unlabeled multi-modal medical images); provided as better-than-original-SAM initialisation for downstream tasks; published in MELBA 2025

nodes.label: PathoSAM Pretrained Weights
nodes.rationale: SAM variant adapted for computational pathology; normalisation: SA-1B statistics (SAM-style); weights hosted via micro-sam util; includes additional instance segmentation decoder; MIDL 2025 Oral; paper: openreview EWGV97ESaP

nodes.label: MedicoSAM Pretrained Weights
nodes.rationale: SAM variant for general medical image segmentation; normalisation: SA-1B statistics (SAM-style); vit_b_medicosam.pt downloadable via wget from owncloud; arxiv 2501.11734

nodes.label: MRI Foundation Pretrained Weights
nodes.rationale: Foundation model specifically pretrained on MRI data; normalisation: min-max [0,1] x 255; downloadable from Google Drive; arxiv 2506.12186

nodes.label: Fine-tuning Architecture Combination Diagram (v3)
nodes.rationale: Visualises the 18 fine-tuning strategy combinations: 3 image encoder architectures (ViT-H depth=32, ViT-B depth=12, ViT-Tiny/MobileSAM depth=4, related by distillation) x 2 model components (finetune encoder+decoder vs decoder-only with frozen encoder) x 3 fine-tuning methods (Vanilla full-parameter update, PEFT-Adapter blocks, PEFT-LoRA blocks)

nodes.label: Fine-tuning Strategy Overview by Dataset Availability (v9)
nodes.rationale: Shows 3 experimental setups on a data-availability spectrum: Setup 1 (single labelled dataset, supervised fine-tuning), Setup 2 (multiple labelled datasets for different tasks, prompt-based fine-tuning with box/point prompts), Setup 3 (multiple unlabelled datasets for task-agnostic self-supervised pretraining + task-specific labelled dataset for supervised fine-tuning)
nodes.norm_label: fine-tuning strategy overview by dataset availability (v9)

nodes.label: Adapter Fine-tuning (PEFT)
nodes.rationale: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning by inserting lightweight adapter modules between transformer blocks; configurable per encoder block range (encoder_adapter_depths); can be applied to encoder, decoder, or both; preserves most original weights frozen

nodes.label: LoRA Fine-tuning (PEFT)
nodes.rationale: Low-Rank Adaptation applied to specified transformer blocks; trainable rank decomposition matrices injected into attention layers; configurable via encoder_lora_layer list; can be applied to encoder, decoder, or both

nodes.label: Vanilla Fine-tuning
nodes.rationale: Full parameter update of either encoder+decoder or decoder-only; controlled by if_update_encoder flag; highest parameter count but may overfit on small datasets
